lines: +    // Build up the prompt following the Mistral Instruct v0.3 format:
+    // Mistral Instruct v0.3 - simple [INST]/[/INST] format without [SYSTEM_PROMPT] token
+    // Detection: has [INST] and [/INST] markers but NOT [SYSTEM_PROMPT] (which belongs to Ministral 3)
+        LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v3\n");

// Note: Mistral Small 3.2 uses [CALL_ID] which Ministral doesn't have, so we can distinguish them

content.text: Now let me verify the changes look correct:

The changes look exactly right. Here's a summary of everything that was added to `common/chat.cpp`:

---

### Changes made to `common/chat.cpp`

**1. New handler function** (`common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3`, lines 1634–1666):

- Initializes `data.prompt` with the BOS token (`<s>`).
- Iterates `inputs.messages` collecting roles via `msg.value("role", "")` and content via `msg.value("content", "")`.
- Stores any `system` message in `system_content` for later use.
- For the first `user` message, prepends the system content (if any) followed by `\n` before the user content, then wraps the whole thing in `[INST] … [/INST]`.
- Subsequent `user` messages get the same `[INST] … [/INST]` wrapping without the system prefix.
- Each `assistant` message is appended as ` {content}{eos_token}` (space + content + `</s>`).
- Sets `data.format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY`.

This produces exactly the required format:
```
<s>[INST] {system}\n{user1} [/INST] {assistant1}</s>[INST] {user2} [/INST]
```

**2. Detection in `common_chat_try_specialized_template()`** (lines 1672–1678):

Added as the **first** check (before the Ministral 3 block) so it takes priority for plain `[INST]`/`[/INST]` templates:
```cpp
if (src.find("[INST]") != std::string::npos && src.find("[/INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```
The `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` exclusion ensures this doesn't accidentally match the Ministral 3 template, which uses `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` instead of prepending the system to the first user turn. No existing code was modified.

The changes look exactly right. Here's a summary of everything that was added to `common/chat.cpp`:

---

### Changes made to `common/chat.cpp`

**1. New handler function** (`common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3`, lines 1634–1666):

- Initializes `data.prompt` with the BOS token (`<s>`).
- Iterates `inputs.messages` collecting roles via `msg.value("role", "")` and content via `msg.value("content", "")`.
- Stores any `system` message in `system_content` for later use.
- For the first `user` message, prepends the system content (if any) followed by `\n` before the user content, then wraps the whole thing in `[INST] … [/INST]`.
- Subsequent `user` messages get the same `[INST] … [/INST]` wrapping without the system prefix.
- Each `assistant` message is appended as ` {content}{eos_token}` (space + content + `</s>`).
- Sets `data.format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY`.

This produces exactly the required format:
```
<s>[INST] {system}\n{user1} [/INST] {assistant1}</s>[INST] {user2} [/INST]
```

**2. Detection in `common_chat_try_specialized_template()`** (lines 1672–1678):

Added as the **first** check (before the Ministral 3 block) so it takes priority for plain `[INST]`/`[/INST]` templates:
```cpp
if (src.find("[INST]") != std::string::npos && src.find("[/INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```
The `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` exclusion ensures this doesn't accidentally match the Ministral 3 template, which uses `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` instead of prepending the system to the first user turn. No existing code was modified.
